  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "EMT bend is kinked and flattened, reducing internal cross-sectional area and damaging conductor insulation during pulling.",
    "Kinked EMT does not provide a smooth, continuous raceway as required by NEC 358.24."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Cut out the defective section of EMT and replace with a properly bent segment using a correct bending tool and technique.",
    "Verify replacement bend radius meets NEC Chapter 9, Table 2 minimums and that no sharp edges or flattening exist."
  ],
  "rationale": "A kinked or flattened EMT bend violates NEC 358.24 (Bends — How Made) which requires bends to be made so the tubing is not damaged and the internal diameter is not effectively reduced. This condition can damage conductor insulation during pulling and impede future conductor replacement, creating a high risk of short circuits or ground faults. The defect is visible and measurable by eye; the bend is clearly out of tolerance.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 358.24",
    "NEC Chapter 9, Table 2"
  ]
}
